name: Coastal roads of Ondarroa, Lekeitio and Ea — circular from Berriatua
name_original: Carreteras costeras de Ondarroa, Lekeitio y Ea — circular desde Berriatua
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Berriatua traverse the diverse landscapes of Bizkaia, offering varied elevations and terrains. The region is characterized by green landscapes, dense forests, and open valleys, with coastal paths providing sea views. Cyclists can expect undulating terrain with both ascents and descents, alongside views of the River Artibai. The area's topography includes numerous hills, making it suitable for different levels of road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Berriatua The most popular road cycling route is Coastal roads of Ondarroa, Lekeitio and Ea — circular from Berriatua, a 38.7 miles (62.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive coastal views and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Lekeitio Old Town and Basilica – Ondarroa Bay loop from Berriatua, a moderate 27.5 miles (44.2 km) path. This route explores coastal towns and offers views of Ondarroa Bay. Local road cyclists also love the Ondarroa Bay – Altzola Church loop from Ondarroa, a 32.4 miles (52.1 km) trail leading through coastal and inland landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Road cycling around Berriatua is defined by green landscapes, coastal paths, and undulating hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult climbs. The routes in Berriatua are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 4000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Berriatua's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Berriatua?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Berriatua, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes vary in difficulty from moderate to difficult, traversing green landscapes, dense forests, and coastal paths with sea views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Berriatua?
A: Road cycling in Berriatua features undulating terrain with both challenging ascents and rewarding descents. The region is characterized by green landscapes, forests, and open valleys, with some routes offering stunning coastal views, particularly around the Asterrika neighborhood and towards towns like Deba and Mutriku.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Berriatua?
A: While many routes in Berriatua involve undulating terrain, there are options suitable for various skill levels. For families or those seeking less strenuous rides, it's best to look for routes categorized as 'moderate' or 'easy' within the komoot guide. These often focus on flatter sections or less demanding climbs.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes around Berriatua offer views of several interesting landmarks and natural features. You might encounter historical sites such as the &lt;strong&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Zenarruza&lt;/strong&gt; or the &lt;strong&gt;Hermitage of Magdalena&lt;/strong&gt;. For stunning vistas, consider routes that pass by viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3562432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendatagaina Viewpoint and Flysch Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/318367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Bizkaia Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Coastal routes also provide panoramic sea views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available from Berriatua?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Berriatua are circular. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031473489/coastal-roads-of-ondarroa-lekeitio-and-ea-circular-from-berriatua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal roads of Ondarroa, Lekeitio and Ea — circular from Berriatua&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive coastal views. Another popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38212277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lekeitio Old Town and Basilica – Ondarroa Bay loop from Berriatua&lt;/a&gt;, which explores coastal towns.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Berriatua?
A: The road cycling routes in Berriatua are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful green landscapes, and the stunning coastal views that make for a rewarding ride.

Q: Which road cycling routes offer the best coastal views?
A: For exceptional coastal views, consider routes that venture towards the Asterrika neighborhood of Berriatua or extend to nearby towns like Ondarroa, Lekeitio, Deba, and Mutriku. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031473489/coastal-roads-of-ondarroa-lekeitio-and-ea-circular-from-berriatua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal roads of Ondarroa, Lekeitio and Ea — circular from Berriatua&lt;/a&gt; is known for its extensive coastal vistas.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Berriatua offers several challenging routes for experienced road cyclists. The region's undulating topography, with numerous hills and significant elevation gains, provides ample opportunity for demanding rides. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031473489/coastal-roads-of-ondarroa-lekeitio-and-ea-circular-from-berriatua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal roads of Ondarroa, Lekeitio and Ea — circular from Berriatua&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ondarroa Bay – Zarautz Beach loop from Ondarroa&lt;/a&gt; are categorized as difficult, featuring substantial distances and elevation changes.

Q: What historical sites can I explore while road cycling near Berriatua?
A: Berriatua and its surroundings are rich in historical heritage. Along your rides, you might encounter sites such as the &lt;strong&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Zenarruza&lt;/strong&gt;, an ancient collegiate church, the 18th-century &lt;strong&gt;Pinta House&lt;/strong&gt;, or the historical &lt;strong&gt;Aranzibia Tower&lt;/strong&gt;. The 16th-century &lt;strong&gt;Hermitage of Magdalena&lt;/strong&gt; and the well-preserved 15th-century &lt;strong&gt;Hermitage of San Lorenzo&lt;/strong&gt; in Asterrika also offer glimpses into the region's past.

Q: Are there any natural features or unique geological formations to see?
A: Beyond the green landscapes and forests, the coastal areas near Berriatua, particularly around Deba and Mutriku, are known for their unique geological formations of exposed rock strata along the beaches. You can also find natural viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3562432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendatagaina Viewpoint and Flysch Cliffs&lt;/a&gt;, offering spectacular natural scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Berriatua?
A: The region's green landscapes suggest a generally mild climate, making road cycling enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be a good time, especially for coastal routes where sea breezes provide relief, though it's always wise to check local weather conditions.

Q: Where can I find more information about cycling in the wider Bizkaia region?
A: For broader information on cycling opportunities within the Bizkaia region, which encompasses Berriatua, you can visit the official tourism website: &lt;a href="https://www.visitbiscay.eus/en/-/cycling-in-bilbao-bizkaia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitbiscay.eus&lt;/a&gt;. This resource can provide additional context on the cycling paradise that Bizkaia offers.
tips: The church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción, the main construction of the urban nucleus, dates from the first part of the 15th century. In it you can see innumerable elements, but the most important is a Romanesque copper cross decorated with precious stones.

Winding section of road between the forest and along the coast. The leafy forests thrive thanks to the humidity spread by the Bay of Biscay. In this section you can pedal pleasantly enjoying the sea breeze through the thick green and glimpse the sea that appears and disappears in the clearings.

Lekeitio is a vibrant and prosperous fishing village famous throughout the Basque Country and Spain. The traditional quality of its fishing, its gastronomy and its beaches live up to its fame. On the clean sand of its beaches and the beauty of its historic quarter, the Basilica of the Assumption of Our Lady stands out, original from the 15th century and in Gothic style. Lekeitio has a great cycling atmosphere as it is frequented by Basque fans who frequent the coast by bicycle.
The ascent to the Bedarona neighborhood is the second notable ascent that cyclists on the short route of the Oiz Challenge must face before heading towards the Balcón de Bizkaia.

Founded by the arrantzales of Bedarona, Ereño and Natxitua, Ea is a rural town that maintains its fishing spirit. The municipality is made up of the urban area of Ea and the neighborhoods, Natxitua, Bedarona and Angelutxu, each with its own characteristics and personality, completing the splendid and diverse landscape.
More info at: https://turismourdaibai.com/es/ea/
The ascent to the Natxitua neighborhood is one of the two most notable ones that cyclists who opt for the most affordable route of the Oiz Challenge must face before heading to the Balcón de Bizkaia.
